comments: computing the change (in %) against previous run

description: warning, not enough data values for safe calculation (MN). Should be at lest 1.
The relative difference between this (L0) and previous (L1, from total of MN) is: /*RESULT*/%

comments: computing the change (in %) against previous runs (is using avg of previous points)

description: warning, not enough data values for safe calculation (MN). Should be at lest 2.
The /*avg*/ is the avg of: ..L1
The relative difference between this (L0) and avg(/*avg*/) of previous(/*len*/) values is: /*RESULT*/%

comments: computing the change (in %) against previous runs (is using geometrical mean of previous points)

description: warning, not enough data values for safe calculation (MN). Should be at lest 2.
The /*geom*/ is the geometrical mean of: ..L1
The relative difference between this (L0) and geom(/*geom*/) of previous(/*len*/) values is: /*RESULT*/%

comments: computing the change (in %) against previous runs with extremes cut-off
is using avgN - average with cutting of N*2 - of previous extreme values
one parameter, cut amount. 1 means cut of biggest and lowest member. 2 mens cut two biggest and 2 lowest. At least one (or two) members of array are always persisted
eg: SHORT_UP_CUT_OK 1  from  24 -5 3 12 will calc avg from 3 and 12 as 24 and -5 will be cut away
eg: SHORT_UP_CUT_OK 100  from  same values will again lead to 3 and 12 as there is nothing more to remove

description: warning, not enough data values for safe calculation (MN). Should be at lest 2*/*1*/+1 (/*cut-comment*/).
The /*avgn*/ is the average value (with /*1*/ lowest and /*1*/ biggest values removed) from: /*sort-comment*/
The relative difference between this (L0) and filtered avg(/*avgn*/) of original MN (after cut: /*len*/) values is: /*RESULT*/%

comments: computing the change (in %) against previous runs with extremes cut-off
is using geomN - geometrical mean with cutting of N*2 - of previous extreme values
one parameter, cut amount. 1 means cut of biggest and lowest member. 2 mens cut two biggest and 2 lowest. At least one (or two) members of array are always persisted
eg: SHORT_BIG_CUT_CALC 1  from  24 -5 3 12 will calc avg from 3 and 12 as 24 and -5 will be cut away
eg: SHORT_BIG_CUT_CALC 100  from  same values will again lead to 3 and 12 as there is nothing more to remove

description: warning, not enough data values for safe calculation (MN). Should be at lest 2*/*1*/+1 (/*cut-comment*/).
The /*geomn*/ is the geometrical-mean value (with /*1*/ lowest and /*1*/ biggest values removed) from: /*sort-comment*/
The relative difference between this (L0) and filtered avg(/*avgn*/) of original MN (after cut: /*len*/) values is: /*RESULT*/%

comments: computing the change (in %) of newer half of builds against older half of builds
is using avg - average - on those two sets of points

description: There is MN of values, each half will have /*len*/ members:  L{0}..L{MN}
warning, only one member. Both halves are same.
/*avg1*/ is avg of upper half (/*len*/-MN) of: L{MN/2}..L{MN}
/*avg2*/ is avg of lower half (0-/*len*/) of: L{0}..L{MN/2}
The relative difference between avgs of lower and upper half of original array is: /*RESULT*/%

comments: computing the change (in %) of newer half of builds against older half of builds
is using geom - geometrical mean - of previous points

description: There is MN of values, each half will have /*len*/ members:  L{0}..L{MN}
warning, only one member. Both halves are same.
/*geom1*/ is geometrical mean of upper half (/*len*/-MN) of: L{MN/2}..L{MN}
/*geom2*/ is geometrical mean of lower half (0-/*len*/) of: L{0}..L{MN/2}
The relative difference between geoms of lower and upper half of original array is: /*RESULT*/%

comments: computing the change (in %) of newer half of builds against older half of builds (both with extremes cut-off)
is using avgN - average with cutting - of previous points
one parameters, cut amount
eg: LONG_CUT_CALC 1  from  24 -5 3 12 will calc avg from 3 and 12 as 24 and -5 will be cut away
eg: LONG_CUT_CALC 100  from  same values will again lead to 3 and 12 as there is nothing more to remove
(avgN(cut,L{MN/2}..L{MN})/(avgN(cut,L0..L{MN/2})/100)-100)

description: warning, not enough data values for safe calculation (MN). Should be at lest 4*/*1*/+1 (/*cut-comment*/).
The /*avgn1*/ is the average value (with /*1*/ lowest and /*1*/ biggest values removed) from: /*sort-comment1*/
The /*avgn2*/ is the average value (with /*1*/ lowest and /*1*/ biggest values removed) from: /*sort-comment2*/
The relative difference between filtered avgs of newer half of data (/*avgn1*/) and older half of data (/*avgn2*/) of original MN (after 2x cut of /*len*/) values is: /*RESULT*/%

comments: computing the change  (in %) of newer half of builds against older half of builds (both with extremes cut-off)
is using geomN - geometrical mean with cutting - of previous points
one parameters, cut amount
eg: LONG_BIG_CUT_CLAC 1  from  24 -5 3 12 will calc avg from 3 and 12 as 24 and -5 will be cut away
eg: LONG_BIG_CUT_CLAC 100  from  same values will again lead to 3 and 12 as there is nothing more to remove
(geomN(cut,L{MN/2}..L{MN})/(geomN(cut,L0..L{MN/2})/100)-100)

description: warning, not enough data values for safe calculation (MN). Should be at lest 4*/*1*/+1 (/*cut-comment*/).
The /*geomn1*/ is the geometric mean (with /*1*/ lowest and /*1*/ biggest values removed) from: /*sort-comment1*/
The /*geomn2*/ is the geometric mean of (with /*1*/ lowest and /*1*/ biggest values removed) from: /*sort-comment2*/
The relative difference between filtered geoms of newer half of data (/*geomn1*/) and older half of data (/*geomn2*/) of original MN (after 2x cut of /*len*/) values is: /*RESULT*/%

comments: checking whether drop (in %) against previous run was not greater then threshold
one parameter, threshold number
eg: IMMEDIATE_UP_OK 5

description: This calculation is ensuring, that 'more is better', on immediate scale with tolerance of /*1*/%.
The result, whether drop (in %) against previous run was not greater then threshold is /*RESULT*/:
The /*immediate_calc*/% is lower or equal to threshold /*1*/%, that is ok.
The /*immediate_calc*/% is greater then threshold /*1*/%, that is bad. You are facing momentary performance regression.

comments: checking whether rise (in %) against previous run was not greater then threshold
one parameter, threshold number
eg: IMMEDIATE_DOWN_OK 5

description: This calculation is ensuring, that 'less is better', on immediate scale with tolerance of /*1*/%.
The result, whether rise (in %) against previous run was not greater then threshold is /*RESULT*/:
The /*immediate_calc*/% is greater or equal to threshold -/*1*/%, that is ok.
The /*immediate_calc*/% is lower then threshold -/*1*/%, that is bad. You are facing momentary performance regression.

comments: checking whether drop (in %) against previous runs was not greater then threshold
is using avg of previous points
one parameter, threshold number
eg: SHORT_UP_OK 5

description: This calculation is ensuring, that 'more is better', against all (avg) previous runs with tolerance of /*1*/%.
The result,  whether drop (in %) against avg of previous run was not greater then threshold is /*RESULT*/:
The /*short_calc*/% is lower or equal to threshold /*1*/%, that is ok.
The /*short_calc*/% is greater then threshold /*1*/%, that is bad. You are facing performance regression.

is using avgN - average with cutting - of previous points
two parameters, cut amount and threshold number
eg: SHORT_UP_CUT_OK 1 5

description: This calculation is ensuring, that 'more is better', against all - with 2*/*1*/ extremes excluded - previous runs with tolerance of /*1*/%.
The result,  whether drop (in %) against avg (with the removed extremes) of previous run was not greater then threshold is /*RESULT*/:
The /*short_cut_calc*/% is lower or equal to threshold /*1*/%, that is ok.

comments: combination:LONG_UP_CUT_OK or SHORT_UP_CUT_OK or IMMEDIATE_UP_OK
four parameters
cut, immediate threshold, short time threshold, long time threshold
eg: FINAL_UP_CUTTING_OK  2 5 5 5

description: In combination `more is better` with cutting of extremes calculated via average values, the result is /*RESULT*/:
All passed (all checks are false). No performance regression noticed.
At least one of them (see above) failed (is true), that is bad. Performance regression detected.
